sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking on the charcoal block. Let me check what changed after clicking on the charcoal block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking on the charcoal block
before_nodes = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after_nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in after_nodes:
    bm = [bn for bn in before_nodes if bn['color'] == n['color'] and bn['hash'] == n['hash']]
    if len(bm) > 0:
        bm0 = bm[0]
        print("Node " + str(n['id']) + " " + n['color'] + ": before " + str(bm0['boundary']) + " -> " + str(n['boundary']))
    else:
        print("Node " + str(n['id']) + " (" + n['color'] + "): NEW")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Interesting! After clicking on the charcoal block didn't move the blue bar didn't move the blue bar. The blue bar is still at [[20, 8], [20, 27], [23, 27], [23, 8]] - same as before. So clicking on the charcoal block did nothing to the blue bar.
So only clicking on the magenta block moves the blue bar. Let me think about the magenta block is the "player" that controls the blue bar. The blue bar moved down-left when I clicked on the magenta block.
Let me think about the game mechanics:
- The magenta block is at rows 4-7, cols 36-39. The charcoal block is at rows 1-3, cols 36-39. The blue bar is at rows 20-23, cols 8-27.
The blue bar moved down 4 rows and left 4 cols when I clicked on the magenta block.
